title: Mowatt-Larssen Succeeds Ryan
summary: Rolf Mowatt-Larssen, a former senior fellow with the Belfer Center and long-time intelligence officer, has been named Director of the Center’s Intelligence and Defense Projects and Project on Saudi and GCC Security.
keywords: Saudi and Gulf Cooperation Council Security
tags: Saudi and Gulf Cooperation Council Security
body: Will Lead Intelligence/Defense and Saudi/Gulf Projects The latest from Belfer See more Rolf Mowatt-Larssen , a former senior fellow with the Belfer Center and long-time intelligence officer, has been named Director of the Center’s Intelligence and Defense Projects and Project on Saudi and GCC Security. He succeeds Brigadier General (ret) Kevin Ryan , who joined the Center in 2005. Mowatt-Larssen has been affiliated with the Center as a senior fellow since 2009, following his service as Director of the Office of Intelligence and Counterintelligence at the U.S. Department of Energy and as head of the Central Intelligence Agency’s WMD and counter-terrorism efforts. Prior to his appointment to the top intelligence position in the Department of Energy, Mowatt-Larssen served for 23 years as a CIA intelligence officer in various domestic and international posts, including as Deputy Associate Director of Central Intelligence for Military Support and Chief of the Weapons of Mass Destruction Department and the Counterterrorism Center. A graduate of the U.S. Military Academy at West Point, Mowatt-Larssen is the recipient of a number of awards including the CIA Director’s Award, Distinguished Career Intelligence Medal, Commendation Medal, and Secretary of Defense Civilian Distinguished Service Medal. Kevin Ryan joined the Belfer Center in 2005 as a senior fellow and in 2009 founded the Center’s U.S.–Russia Initiative to Prevent Nuclear Terrorism. He served as the Center’s Executive Director for Research from 2010 to 2013, at which time he became founding Director of the Center’s Intelligence and Defense Projects. Ryan will continue as an associate at the Center working on intelligence and defense issues with a focus on Russian military and security issues. He will also remain, along with Mowatt-Larssen, part of The Elbe Group, which Ryan launched in 2010 to maintain open communications between high-level defense and intelligence officials in the U.S. and Russia. “We will greatly miss interacting with Kevin on a daily basis,” said Belfer Center Director Graham Allison , “but we are fortunate to have Rolf taking his space as head of our intelligence and defense and Saudi/GCC initiatives and as manager of our Recanati-Kaplan Foundation Fellows Program. We have benefited for a number of years from Rolf’s insights on intelligence, nuclear security, and other issues, and look forward to having him here full-time in his new position.” “I am looking forward to continuing the cutting-edge work of the Belfer Center,” Mowatt-Larssen said, “bringing intelligence and defense issues of policy relevance into focus for students, experts and researchers, in the U.S. and internationally.” Recommended citation "Mowatt-Larssen Succeeds Ryan." Belfer Center Newsletter, Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School (Summer 2017). _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: Rolf Mowatt-Larssen, a former senior fellow and intelligence officer, has been appointed Director of the Belfer Center's Intelligence and Defense Projects and Project on Saudi and GCC Security, succeeding Brigadier General (ret) Kevin Ryan. Mowatt-Larssen brings extensive experience from his roles as Director of the Office of Intelligence and Counterintelligence at the U.S. Department of Energy and as head of CIA's WMD and counter-terrorism efforts, following a 23-year career at the CIA. Kevin Ryan, who founded the Center's Intelligence and Defense Projects and the U.S.-Russia Initiative to Prevent Nuclear Terrorism, will continue as an associate focusing on Russian military and security issues and remain part of The Elbe Group. Belfer Center Director Graham Allison expressed confidence in Mowatt-Larssen's ability to lead the initiatives and manage the Recanati-Kaplan Foundation Fellows Program, highlighting his insights on intelligence and nuclear security.
result.main_content_zh: 前高级研究员兼情报官员罗尔夫·莫瓦特-拉森被任命为贝尔弗科学与国际事务中心情报与防御项目及沙特与海湾合作委员会安全项目主任，接替退役准将凯文·瑞安。莫瓦特-拉森曾担任美国能源部情报与反情报办公室主任，并领导中央情报局的大规模杀伤性武器和反恐工作，拥有23年的中情局职业生涯，经验丰富。凯文·瑞安于2005年加入中心，创立了情报与防御项目以及美俄防止核恐怖主义倡议，他将继续作为中心副研究员，专注于俄罗斯军事与安全问题，并保留在旨在维持美俄高层防务与情报官员沟通的易北河小组中的成员身份。贝尔弗中心主任格雷厄姆·艾利森对莫瓦特-拉森领导相关倡议和管理Recanati-Kaplan基金会研究员项目的能力表示信心，并强调其在情报和核安全领域的见解。
keywords_zh: 沙特与海湾合作委员会安全
